page: Amazon Web Services m5n.8xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 3.1 GHz. The configuration includes 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs with hyperthreading enabled, supported by a 35 MB L3 cache. Memory capacity stands at 128.0 GB of DDR4 RAM running at 3 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of memory per core. The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps and does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates average multi-core performance across most compute and memory workloads. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test, Geekbench multi-core score, and PassMark CPU score all fall within the middle 50% tier. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression scoring in the bottom 25% tier. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks consistently register in the average tier, as do database operations and Redis SET operations.

For specialized tasks, the instance handles small to medium language model inference at average speeds, but drops to the bottom 10% tier for large 70B models. Given the dedicated CPU allocation and balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the m5n.8xlarge offers stable resource density for general-purpose workloads. It is suited for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, software compilation, and database operations that rely on network-attached storage rather than local NVMe drives.
description: Amazon Web Services m5n.8xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose server instance optimized for network and EBS performance. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz, it features 32 vCPUs (16 physical cores) and 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps, though it lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Benchmark results place its multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance consistently in the average tier, while single-core execution speeds represent a tradeoff, falling into the bottom 25% tier. This hardware profile is suited for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, static web serving, and moderate database operations that utilize network-attached storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering average multi-core performance for network-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.8xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose server with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A network-optimized general-purpose server featuring thirty-two virtual cores, balanced memory density, and twenty-five gigabits of baseline network bandwidth.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores
128.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 4.0 GB per core
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with EBS optimization
Average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Tradeoff of weaker single-core performance in the bottom 25% tier
Suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, and database operations
